preview_text:
The model favors Uzbekistan at 46%, while DR Congo is given only a 24% chance of victory. The underdog's path to three points remains available but requires overcoming a substantial gap in the forecast.

The 30% draw probability suggests a competitive match with realistic prospect of a stalemate, though Uzbekistan's win probability exceeds both other outcomes. The gap between Uzbekistan and a DR Congo victory spans roughly two percentage points in either direction from a draw—a moderately unbalanced matchup rather than an even contest.

The market consensus diverges sharply from the model. Sportsbooks place DR Congo's win probability at 44%, nearly double the model's 24%, while pricing Uzbekistan at 29%—17 percentage points below the model's 46%. Polymarket pricing aligns more closely with sportsbooks on both teams' win odds, though agreeing on the draw at roughly 25-27%. This represents a material disagreement on which team holds the edge; the dashboard offers the model's view without claiming superiority over market judgment.
